qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Collaborator privilege escalation via collab add allows ReadWriteAccess user to grant AdminAccess
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets repo collaborator authorization for granting AdminAccess, not the advisory's repo import source-remote authorization flaw that clones server-local private repositories.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via repo import remote URL bypassing SSRF protection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding shares the repo import to git.Clone sink, but it identifies an internal-network URL SSRF and missing SSRF validation rather than the advisory's server-local filesystem path import and missing source-repository authorization/private-repo boundary.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause: authenticated repo import of a server-local bare repository path, with authorization checked only for the attacker-controlled destination repository and not for the private source repository.
evidence: The advisory describes pkg/ssh/cmd/import.go passing REMOTE to pkg/backend/repo.go git.Clone after destination-only authorization, allowing a low-privilege SSH user to import $DATA_PATH/repos/secret.git and read another user's private repository.

The referenced fix c147421caf234bcfc1570c79d728ecbbe5813e55 adds validateImportRemote() and ErrInvalidRemote: 'remote must be a network URL', with a regression test rejecting repo import of $DATA_PATH/repos/secret.git.
collab-privilege-escalation concerns repo collab add and privilege escalation from ReadWriteAccess to AdminAccess, a different command and authorization boundary.

ssrf-via-repo-import concerns git:// or http:// internal network remotes and bypassing SSRF protection; its report and PoC do not identify server-local filesystem path cloning or unauthorized disclosure of another user's private Soft Serve repository.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so Tier1/Tier2/Tier3 do not apply.
